items.title: Ridge and Lasso
items.description: Ridge (L2) and Lasso (L1) are the canonical linear-regression algorithms that instantiate these penalties on ||y - Xβ||₂². This topic owns the overfitting-mitigation story and penalty geometry; Ridge and Lasso own closed-form solutions, solvers, and API usage.

items.title: Variance
items.description: L1/L2 regularization lowers Var(ŷ) across training samples by restricting weight magnitude—trading controlled bias for variance reduction. Variance owns the formal decomposition; this topic owns the penalty lever.

items.title: Underfitting vs overfitting
items.description: Apply regularization when train error is low and validation error is high; ease λ when both errors are high (underfitting or excessive regularization). Underfitting vs Overfitting owns the train–val diagnostic workflow.

items.title: Loss functions
items.description: Regularization adds λ Ω(θ) to the data loss L_data—MSE for regression, cross-entropy for classification. Loss Functions owns L_data definitions; this topic owns the penalty term Ω.

items.title: Cross-validation
items.description: λ is selected by minimizing cross-validated error, not training loss. Cross-validation owns the resampling protocol for hyperparameter search.
